titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76750

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=678 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:50:03.945869+00 | translation moa=679 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:50:02.175966+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis Robertus de Quinci salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me dedisse et concessisse et hac mea karta
confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte crucis de Edenburh et Canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus unam Carucatam terre et decem
acras interritorio de trauernent uersus Phaeside pro excambio illius Carucate terre de prestun quam Canonici predicti uersis me
et Monachos de Neubotle Calumpniauerunt ita quod prenominati Canonici me et heredes meos et predictos Monachos
super illa Calumpnia predicte terre de prestun quietos clamauerunt pro predicto excambio . Hinc est quod ego Robertus de
Quinci dedi et concessi et hac karta mea confirmaui predictis Canonicis prenominatam terram cum decem acris terre te
nendam in puram elemosinam et perpetuam de me et heredibus meis cum communi pastura hominium meorum de
trauernent ad tantum terre pertinenti et cum libero exitu ad predictam communem pasturam adeo libere et quiete et
honorifice sicut liberius et quietius et honorificentius aliqua elemosina in Regno Regis scocie tenetur uel possidetur .
His testibus Symone Canonico de bethleem Willelmo Capellano de trauernent Ricardo Ridel Radulfo de Graam
pagano de hetleia . Willelmo de Graam Simone de tunderley . Dauid de lassewade Alvredo de cranestun
Alano et stephano de Phaeside . Laurentio et Roberto de boneire . et Bartholomeo burgensibus de
edenesburch . Gileberto clerico . Michaele flandrensi . et Aliis multis .

p.cl: To all Holy Mother Church's sons, Robert de Quincy [sends] greeting.

Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to God, the church of the Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God in that place, one ploughgate of land and ten acres in the territory of Tranent, in the direction of Fa'side, for the exchange of that ploughgate of land at Preston which the canons aforesaid claimed against me and the monks of Newbattle, so that the aforenamed canons quitclaimed me and my heirs and the aforesaid monks over that claim of the aforesaid land of Preston in return for the aforesaid exchange. That is why I, Robert de Quincy, gave, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to the aforesaid canons the aforenamed land with ten acres of land, to be held in pure and everlasting alms of me and my heirs, with my men of Tranent's common pasture pertaining to so much land, and with free right of egress towards the aforesaid common pasture; as freely, quitly, and honourably as any alms is most freely, quitly, honourably held and possessed in the kingdom of the king of Scotland.
